message:
Add early guard for tilde expansion when remote home is unavailable

The sync_path_rsync() and sync_path_scp() methods now check if the remote
path starts with '~' when remote_home is None, returning an immediate
failure instead of attempting the operation.

Previously, if the remote home directory detection failed (e.g., SSH
connection issues, restricted shell), paths containing '~' would be
passed unexpectedly to rsync/scp, potentially syncing from the wrong
location or failing with cryptic errors.

Now returns a clear error message:
"Cannot expand '~' in remote path; failed to determine remote home directory"

This makes the failure mode explicit and provides actionable information
to the user about what went wrong.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
